title: Add autoregressive generation and the sample CLI smoke test
description:
Add shared no-cache autoregressive generation and turn the sample script into a checkpoint-driven command.

Plan ticket coverage: MODEL-011, SMOKE-006, SMOKE-008.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generation crops to context length and appends exactly max_new_tokens.
- Temperature, top-k, and seeded reproducibility are tested.
- Generated IDs remain valid and byte-decode without error.
- A subprocess test loads a temporary tiny checkpoint and prints non-empty text.

title: Decompose the remaining tracking foundation and integrations
description:
Progressively decompose the remaining TRACK-001 and TRACK-005 through TRACK-034 scope after Milestone 1 is complete; do not implement the whole range in this planning bead.

Plan ticket coverage: TRACK-001 and TRACK-005 through TRACK-034.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Review the current phase plan, completed code, cross-cutting tests, and metric contracts before defining work.
- Create coherent independently testable PR-sized child beads and preserve every listed source code exactly once.
- Give each child concrete acceptance criteria and only genuine technical dependencies.
- Before closing this planning bead, attach the new delivery beads as blockers of Milestone 2; the milestone must not become ready merely because planning finished.
- Run bd dep cycles and confirm the newly exposed ready set remains small and actionable.
- Assign later tokenizer, data, base, SFT, ChatCORE, and inference tracking codes to the milestone they actually support rather than forcing all of Phase 10 into Milestone 2.

title: Add the smoke checkpoint and resume contract
description:
Add the minimal checkpoint contract omitted from the formal Sprint ticket range but required by its exit criteria.

Plan ticket coverage: TRAIN-007, TRAIN-008.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- last.pt atomically records model, optimizer, scheduler, resolved config, step, and byte-tokenizer metadata.
- A shared loader reconstructs the sampling model.
- Resumed training advances from the recorded next step instead of restarting.
- A bounded CPU test covers save, load, and resume.
- Exact RNG and dataloader continuity remain assigned to TRAIN-009 and TRAIN-010.

title: Log core training metrics and run side-effect-free validation
description:
Emit first-sprint metrics through the tracker and provide a reusable validation hook that cannot mutate training state.

Plan ticket coverage: TRAIN-005, TRAIN-006.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Metrics include monotonic step plus train/loss, train/lrm, train/dt, train/grad_norm, and epoch when available.
- Validation uses inference/no-grad behavior, makes no optimizer/scheduler action, and leaves parameters/gradients unchanged.
- Validation aggregates loss correctly and restores the previous model mode.
- The smoke run writes valid metrics.jsonl under the run directory.

title: Verify pretrain to checkpoint, sample, metrics, and resume
description:
Compose the first-sprint pieces into the exact command-level workflow and one bounded CPU integration test.

Plan ticket coverage: First Sprint Exit Criteria and cross-cutting smoke tests.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- python -m scripts.pretrain --config configs/smoke.yaml lowers loss and writes resolved config, metrics.jsonl, and last.pt.
- python -m scripts.sample --checkpoint runs/smoke/checkpoints/last.pt emits non-empty decodable text.
- Resume advances the saved step.
- pytest covers seed reproducibility, accumulation equivalence, eval-no-update, JSONL validity, overfit, and generation.
- PR evidence includes a fixed-seed sample for the subjective non-random-ish check.

title: Build a Chat LLM From Scratch in PyTorch
description:
Deliver the educational, nanochat-aligned, end-to-end chat-LLM pipeline in staged vertical slices on one RTX 3090, while keeping core tokenizer, data, model, training, evaluation, and inference mechanics understandable and testable.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Milestones 1 through 10 are completed in order, with their delivery evidence recorded.
- Phases 0 through 14 are complete; the optional Phase 15 work is either completed or explicitly deferred/superseded by a recorded decision.
- The final system covers tokenization, pretraining, base evaluation, SFT, chat evaluation, inference, CLI, and a local-only web UI.
- Local JSONL metrics remain available for every applicable run and the documented single-RTX-3090 constraints are respected.
notes: Organizational roadmap container; executable work is tracked in child beads.

title: Implement manual causal attention with a mask regression
description:
Implement explicit QKV attention, scaling, lower-triangular masking, softmax, and output projection before optimized backends.

Plan ticket coverage: MODEL-004, MODEL-013.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Input/output shape is (B,T,C) and gradients remain finite.
- A deterministic tensor case matches a simple reference implementation.
- Changing future tokens cannot affect earlier outputs.
- Invalid head dimensions and over-context sequences fail clearly.
notes: Implementation is review-ready. Summary: added a readable manual CausalSelfAttention with combined QKV projection, per-head reshaping, scaled dot-product scores, an explicit registered lower-triangular mask, attention and output dropout, and output projection; added clear construction and context validation plus deterministic reference-parity, finite-gradient, future-token isolation, invalid-head, and over-context regressions. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-4r6-manual-causal-attention. Commit: 1b88963945e01c448d9ef56eddf81578f81ccc29. Validation: focused attention suite passed (5 tests); full pytest suite passed (135 tests); Ruff check passed; targeted Ruff format check passed for both changed files; mypy passed for src, tests, and scripts; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced the sdist and wheel, and wheel inspection confirmed scratch_llm/attention.py is packaged; git diff --check passed. Integration: no separate integration suite or GitHub Actions status checks exist; the full repository pytest suite is the applicable integration run and passed. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/8. Risks/follow-up: the repository-wide Ruff format check flags unchanged baseline files src/scratch_llm/utils.py and tests/test_utils.py; follow-up is tracked as gpt-training-sandbox-i7e and excluded from this scoped PR. No known in-scope risks. Leave this bead in_progress until PR #8 is merged.
close_reason: Merged via PR #8 (https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/8) as ce4025502045ba8b84390b47b137f59de2a734c5; implementation and validation evidence are preserved in the bead notes.

title: Bootstrap run directories and basic logging
description:
Create the shared run bootstrap that owns run layout, resolved-config snapshots, and console/file logging without absorbing metric tracking.

Plan ticket coverage: FOUND-007, FOUND-008.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- A run creates runs/<run.name>/metrics and checkpoints under the configured base output directory.
- The fully resolved config is written atomically and conflicting reruns do not silently overwrite it.
- Unsafe run names and path traversal are rejected.
- Console and run-local file logging initialize idempotently without duplicate handlers.
- Temporary-directory tests cover layout, snapshots, conflicts, and log output.
notes: Hydra revisit note (from gpt-training-sandbox-5hm): the config layer now uses OmegaConf directly. While implementing run bootstrap, explicitly evaluate whether Hydra should replace any run-directory/logging responsibilities rather than layering duplicate behavior on top. Keep OmegaConf-only unless config groups, launchers, or multirun are genuinely needed; if Hydra is adopted later, define chdir/output-directory ownership explicitly.

title: Load, validate, and override YAML configurations
description:
Add safe YAML loading, typed construction, dotted CLI overrides, resolved-config output, and the byte-tokenizer smoke preset.

Plan ticket coverage: FOUND-004, FOUND-005, FOUND-012, SMOKE-004.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Precedence is defaults, then YAML, then repeated dotted CLI overrides.
- Unknown keys, malformed paths, bad types, and invalid resolved values fail with actionable paths.
- configs/smoke.yaml resolves to the byte vocabulary, 2 layers, 2 heads, 128 embedding width, short context, tied weights, and CPU-safe settings.
- Resolved configuration dumps and reloads identically.
- Tests cover partial YAML, precedence, scalar/list overrides, errors, and smoke loading.
notes:
Implementation revised to OmegaConf structured configs and awaiting review.

Summary: OmegaConf 2.4.0.dev13 now owns the typed schema, safe YAML loading, source precedence, ordered dot-list overrides, interpolation resolution, compatible scalar coercion, dataclass instantiation, and YAML output. struct=True preserves unknown-field rejection. The hand-written _construct_config and conversion/reflection helpers were removed; dataclass validation continues to enforce domain and cross-field invariants. The CPU-safe byte-tokenizer smoke preset and source-distribution packaging remain unchanged.
Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-5hm-config-loading
Commits: 54af8dc (initial implementation), c6d5d4a (stable OmegaConf refactor), 6e52092 (structured prerelease simplification)
Validation: uv run --extra dev pytest -q (93 passed); ruff check passed; ruff format --check passed; mypy passed; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced the source distribution and wheel. Wheel metadata declares omegaconf==2.4.0.dev13 and the source distribution contains configs/smoke.yaml.
Integration: No separate integration suite is configured; gh pr checks 6 reports no GitHub branch checks.
PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/6
Hydra follow-up: revisit notes remain on gpt-training-sandbox-59l (run bootstrap), gpt-training-sandbox-2vc (3090 scaling decomposition), and gpt-training-sandbox-143 (architecture experiment decomposition).
Risks/follow-up: Upgrade the exact prerelease pin when OmegaConf 2.4 becomes stable. OmegaConf 2.4.0.dev13 cannot combine int with Literal["auto"] in one union, so train.grad_accum_steps is annotated int | str while existing runtime validation permits only a positive integer or "auto". Leave this bead in_progress until the PR is merged.
Stable-upgrade follow-up: gpt-training-sandbox-5cf is deferred until 2026-08-21 to replace the prerelease pin and retest the precise grad_accum_steps Literal union.
close_reason: Merged via PR #6 (https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/6) as f734e39afe6c6eac7ebcc06171489cd92fe6a6f0; implementation and validation evidence are preserved in the bead notes.

title: Bootstrap the installable package and test harness
description:
Establish the src-layout package, project metadata, core and optional dependency groups, script/test packages, and the first executable pytest harness.

Plan ticket coverage: FOUND-001, FOUND-002, FOUND-013.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
design: Use Hatchling for distribution scratch-llm. Package src/scratch_llm and the root scripts package so editable and wheel installs expose both. Support Python >=3.10, pin local development to Python 3.12, and commit uv.lock. Core dependencies are torch, numpy, pandas, pyarrow, PyYAML, tqdm, and regex. Keep tracking, web, demo, and dev extras optional per the roadmap; pytest is dev-only. Add pytest import/layout and metadata coverage, validate an isolated core-only editable install from outside the repository, and inspect wheel contents. Ignore generated data, run, and metrics artifacts without creating empty placeholders. Exclude config, utility, script-stub, and README work owned by later beads.
acceptance_criteria:
- pyproject.toml installs scratch_llm from src/ with the planned core dependencies.
- tracking, web, demo, and dev extras are optional; heavyweight training frameworks are absent from core dependencies.
- scratch_llm and scripts import after editable installation with core dependencies only.
- pytest discovers and passes an import/layout smoke test.
- Generated data, run outputs, and empty placeholder directories are not committed.
notes: Implementation is complete and review-ready. Summary: added Hatchling packaging for scratch_llm and scripts, planned core and optional dependency groups, Python 3.12 pin plus uv.lock, pytest layout/metadata coverage, generated-artifact ignores, and scoped wheel/sdist contents. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-5kb-bootstrap. Commit: ad0e6b6472db6ca76edf0ca8ea06a96203d145a6. Validation: pytest passed 3 tests on Python 3.12 and isolated Python 3.10; isolated core-only imports passed with optional/forbidden frameworks absent; Ruff check and format check passed; mypy passed; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced the sdist and wheel; archive contents and ignore behavior were inspected. Integration: no separate integration suite or GitHub status checks exist yet. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/1. Risks/follow-up: README setup and executable script stubs remain intentionally assigned to later foundation work. Local main housekeeping commit 961bd74 was not pushed and is intentionally absent from this bead PR.
close_reason: PR #1 merged into main as a076692127e0715ce847221054b4755b40cb818b; implementation and validation evidence are recorded in the bead notes.

title: Establish the Tracker contract and local backends
description:
Define the tracker API and implement no-op and newline-delimited JSON backends without any W&B dependency.

Plan ticket coverage: TRACK-002, TRACK-003, TRACK-004.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- The log, log_config, log_artifact, and finish methods have one documented contract.
- NullTracker is side-effect free.
- JsonlTracker creates its parent, appends parseable records, preserves values/optional steps, and flushes safely.
- Table-driven tests cover JSONL validity, append behavior, and finish idempotence.

title: Implement the GPT MLP and transformer block
description:
Add GELU feed-forward layers, LayerNorm, residual connections, and dropout around attention and MLP sublayers.

Plan ticket coverage: MODEL-005, MODEL-006.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- MLP and block preserve (B,T,C) shape over multiple tested shapes.
- Both residual paths participate in gradients.
- Dropout is active only in training mode.
- The documented pre-LayerNorm ordering is tested.
notes: Implementation is review-ready. Summary: added a GELU MLP with an explicit positive GPTConfig.mlp_ratio that controls only the temporary hidden width before projection back to n_embd; added a pre-LayerNorm transformer block whose attention and MLP skip connections are named explicitly in forward; documented the distinction between residual-stream and internal MLP widths in the roadmap and smoke config; added regressions for configurable expansion, multiple B,T,C shapes, attention/MLP branch gradients, train-versus-eval dropout, LayerNorm presence, and exact pre-LayerNorm residual ordering. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-6qa-gpt-block. Initial commit: b462a87b5f85ace6ed76b2091b6f4fb28e36a8e4. Review update/current head: 5e2c19cbe3f17d4c7b0e173dfeb87a5c02169f82. Validation: focused config/loading/model suite passed (52 tests); full pytest suite passed (147 tests); Ruff check passed; targeted Ruff format check passed for all changed Python files; mypy passed for src, tests, and scripts; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced the sdist and wheel; git diff --check passed. Integration: no separate integration suite or GitHub status checks exist; the full repository pytest suite is the applicable integration run and passed. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/9. Risks/follow-up: repository-wide Ruff format check still flags unchanged baseline files src/scratch_llm/utils.py and tests/test_utils.py; follow-up is already tracked as gpt-training-sandbox-i7e and excluded from this scoped PR. No known in-scope risks. Leave this bead in_progress until PR #9 is merged.
close_reason: Merged via PR #9 (https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/9) as 02ab36aaacd1b02db4b42d64241c7fc7b3d70623; implementation and validation evidence are preserved in the bead notes.

title: Add token-budget accumulation and gradient clipping
description:
Implement model-independent single-GPU step mechanics so tiny training uses exact token-budget accumulation and one correctly ordered clipping/optimizer step.

Plan ticket coverage: TRAIN-002, TRAIN-004.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- The accumulation count is derived from device batch, sequence length, and total token budget.
- Non-positive or non-divisible budgets fail rather than truncate silently.
- Micro-loss scaling, one optimizer/zero action per window, and clipping order are tested.
- A deterministic CPU test approximately matches the equivalent larger batch and reports grad norm.

title: Define the typed project configuration schema
description:
Implement typed dataclasses for the roadmap configuration, including GPTConfig, deterministic defaults, serialization, and early contradiction checks.

Plan ticket coverage: FOUND-003, MODEL-001.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Typed schemas cover run, tracking, data, tokenizer, model, train, generation, and web.
- GPTConfig exposes the planned dimensions and tied-weight settings.
- Serialization to a plain dictionary loses no configured values.
- Validation rejects non-positive dimensions/batches, n_embd not divisible by n_head, invalid modes, and documented contradictions with dotted-path errors.
- Tests cover defaults, nested serialization, and validation failures.
notes: Implementation is review-ready. Summary: added roadmap-aligned typed dataclasses for all eight configuration sections, GPT dimensions and architecture flags, deep plain-dictionary serialization, deterministic mutable defaults, and dotted-path validation for field and cross-section contradictions. Review update: runtime choice sets are derived from the public Literal aliases via typing.get_args, keeping each option list in one source of truth. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-7o9-config-schema. Initial commit: 42e2522429614ae887d6b2e2cbfec5a18c9aded8. Previous review commit: 38b22972364e8d0314963080fe199741bf081935. Current PR head: 581aaf15161827e0984b0afb059b1c57df608836. Validation: full pytest suite passed (26 tests); Ruff check and format check passed; mypy passed; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced sdist and wheel; wheel inspection confirmed scratch_llm/config.py is packaged. Integration: no separate integration suite or GitHub status checks exist yet; the full repository pytest suite passed on the PR branch. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/3. Risks/follow-up: YAML construction, unknown-key/type handling, CLI overrides, and smoke config files remain intentionally assigned to gpt-training-sandbox-5hm. Leave this bead in_progress until PR #3 is merged. title: Add the tiny-text training path and overfit regression
description:
Compose tokenizer, dataset, model loss, AdamW, accumulation, and clipping into the minimal educational CPU training path.

Plan ticket coverage: SMOKE-005, SMOKE-007, MODEL-014.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- A CPU smoke run performs forward, backward, clipping, optimizer, and scheduler actions correctly.
- With a fixed seed, loss decreases on the tiny fixture.
- One fixed batch reaches a documented deterministic overfit threshold.
- A CUDA smoke case runs conditionally when CUDA is available.

title: Assemble embeddings and the GPT decoder
description:
Build the decoder wrapper from learned token/position embeddings, transformer blocks, final normalization, and an optionally tied LM head.

Plan ticket coverage: MODEL-002, MODEL-003, MODEL-007, MODEL-008.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Forward without targets returns logits shaped (B,T,V).
- Position indices are bounded by context length.
- Tied mode shares the embedding/LM-head parameter and untied mode does not.
- Parameter-count sanity and state-dict save/load equivalence pass.

title: Implement the byte-tokenizer contract
description:
Implement UTF-8 byte encoding with the nine locked special tokens at stable IDs above 255 and no unknown-token path.

Plan ticket coverage: SMOKE-001.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- ASCII, Unicode, emoji, Korean, code, math, and whitespace round-trip exactly.
- Special tokens encode/decode correctly and no pad token is introduced.
- Vocabulary size is 265 and every emitted ID is in range.
- Invalid IDs and unsupported special-token use fail clearly.
notes: Implementation is review-ready. Summary: added a deterministic UTF-8 ByteTokenizer with byte IDs 0-255, the nine locked nanochat special tokens at IDs 256-264, explicit special-token prepend/append support, replacement decoding for malformed generated UTF-8, and clear validation for invalid IDs, types, and unsupported specials. Configuration defaults now reuse the tokenizer special-token source of truth. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-c9s-byte-tokenizer. Commit: 61070d5146d89386ea70b25797f7435acf79a224. Validation: full pytest suite passed (56 tests); Ruff check and format check passed; mypy passed; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced the sdist and wheel; archive inspection confirmed scratch_llm/tokenizer.py and its tests are packaged. Integration: no separate integration suite or GitHub status checks exist yet; the full repository pytest suite passed on the PR branch, and the later bounded Sprint 1 integration path remains tracked by gpt-training-sandbox-z3x. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/4. Risks/follow-up: ordinary encode deliberately treats special-token-looking text as UTF-8 bytes, so callers must use encode_special or prepend/append for control tokens; BPE training and tokenizer artifact save/load remain intentionally assigned to Phase 3. Leave this bead in_progress until PR #4 is merged.
close_reason: Merged via PR #4 (https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/4) as 889be46c38e306f36abdc233fcafed1b5bdba5a1; implementation and validation evidence are preserved in the bead notes.

title: Add the tiny UTF-8 fixture and next-token dataset
description:
Add a small synthetic or clearly public-domain UTF-8 fixture and a deterministic fixed-window next-token dataset.

Plan ticket coverage: SMOKE-002, SMOKE-003.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- The fixture is non-empty valid UTF-8 and has clear provenance.
- Dataset samples are torch.long tensors with shape (seq_len,).
- Targets are the one-token shift of inputs and all IDs are valid.
- Length, boundary, and batched (B,T) behavior are tested.
notes: Implementation is review-ready. Summary: added a synthetic 708-byte UTF-8 fixture with explicit repository-authored provenance; added a deterministic sliding-window NextTokenDataset that emits torch.long input/target pairs, validates sequence length, vocabulary size, token IDs, and index boundaries; added deterministic DataLoader batch coverage; and included fixture data in source distributions. Review update: rewrote the final bounds guard as explicit lower- and upper-bound failure cases for readability. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-cu1-tiny-dataset. Implementation commit: 81c8a7f3cb3a3a9e3de84e03800addd27939b1e0. Latest commit: c66513da7f10427c2b8b2b26e7430061830a8bc6. Validation: focused data tests passed (20 tests); full pytest suite passed (76 tests); Ruff check and format check passed; mypy passed for src, tests, and scripts; uv lock --check passed; uv build produced the sdist and wheel; archive inspection confirmed the fixture, provenance, data module, and tests are in the sdist and the data module is in the wheel. Integration: no separate integration suite or GitHub status checks exist yet; the full repository pytest suite passed on the updated PR branch. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/5. Risks/follow-up: windows intentionally overlap by one start position for deterministic tiny-corpus training; scalable random-offset loading and document-boundary-aware packing remain assigned to later data-pipeline work. Leave this bead in_progress until PR #5 is merged.

title: Add deterministic common runtime utilities
description:
Implement deterministic seeding, device selection, parameter counting, formatting, JSON/atomic writes, and timing helpers used by model and training work.

Plan ticket coverage: FOUND-006, MODEL-012.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Python, NumPy, PyTorch, and available CUDA RNGs are seeded reproducibly.
- Device autodetection works without a GPU and unavailable explicit CUDA requests fail clearly.
- Total/trainable parameter counts handle tied parameters without double counting.
- Formatting and JSON helpers are deterministic and boundary tested.
- Atomic writes replace safely and clean temporary state on failure.
- Timer tests use a monotonic clock without long sleeps.
notes: Implementation complete; awaiting PR review. Summary: added scratch_llm.utils with validated cross-library seeding, CPU/CUDA/MPS device resolution, tied-weight-safe total/trainable parameter counting, deterministic decimal/binary formatting, canonical JSON I/O, same-directory atomic replacement with failure cleanup, and monotonic Timer/context helpers. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-sv0-runtime-utils. Commit: 68209d1c772ac77540d9eeef1ca18e307f88cc27. Validation: uv run pytest (130 passed, including real available-CUDA stream reproducibility); uv run ruff check . (passed); uv run mypy src tests scripts (13 source files passed); uv build (sdist and wheel passed, utils module included). Integration: no project integration-test suite or PR checks exist; full unit/lint/type/build gates are applicable. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/7. Risks/follow-up: no known in-scope risks; bead intentionally remains in_progress until the PR is merged.

title: Add dry-run script interfaces and setup documentation

acceptance_criteria:
- All roadmap script modules exist, including the Sprint-compatible sample command.
- Config commands accept --config, repeated overrides, and --dry-run.
- The pretrain smoke dry-run exits zero, reports resolved config/run paths, and performs no training.
- Every --help path works without optional W&B, web, or demo dependencies.

- README setup, pytest, dry-run, pretrain, and sample commands match subprocess-tested behavior.

title: Decompose pre-training roadmap into Beads
description: Convert plans/pre-training.md into a progressive Beads roadmap: one root epic, phase and milestone epics for the full plan, and coherent testable PR-sized implementation beads for the First Sprint. Preserve source ticket codes, add real dependencies and milestone gates, validate the ready queue and graph, then sync the Dolt database to the remote.
acceptance_criteria: The database contains the complete phase/milestone hierarchy, detailed First Sprint PR-sized beads with acceptance criteria and source-code traceability, an acyclic dependency graph with a small actionable ready set, and the resulting Dolt state is pushed successfully.
notes:
Roadmap graph created from plans/pre-training.md. Root epic: gpt-training-sandbox-3fg. Exact numbered-ticket coverage: 245/245, each owned once by a detailed First Sprint bead or a gated future-decomposition bead.
Validated roadmap: 64 roadmap issues (16 phase epics, 10 milestone gates, 20 detailed First Sprint beads, 17 gated future-decomposition beads, and 1 root epic), 83 blocker edges, 63 parent-child links, and no dependency cycles. All 64 issues have acceptance criteria and unique plan:* external references. Organizational epics are deferred containers so plain bd ready returns only gpt-training-sandbox-5kb, the first executable bead.
Authorized Dolt remote sync completed successfully to origin.
close_reason: Roadmap decomposed, validated, and synced to the remote.

title: Restore repository-wide Ruff format check
description: The gpt-training-sandbox-4r6 quality-gate run found that the command uv run ruff format --check . requests formatting changes in unchanged baseline files src/scratch_llm/utils.py and tests/test_utils.py. Resolve the baseline formatting drift without mixing it into an unrelated feature PR, and determine whether Ruff should be pinned if formatter-version drift is the cause.
acceptance_criteria:
- The command uv run ruff format --check . passes from a clean checkout.
- Any formatter version policy needed for reproducibility is documented or pinned.
- Existing tests, lint, and type checks remain green.

title: Document post-merge bead cleanup workflow
description: Update AGENTS.md so that when the user reports a pull request has been reviewed and merged, the agent performs the verified cleanup and bead-close sequence demonstrated after PR #1. This is a user-requested workflow-maintenance task, not roadmap implementation.
design: Add a Post-Merge Cleanup subsection immediately after the review handoff. Treat the users merged notification as authority for local bead closeout, scoped Beads metadata commits, and merged feature worktree and branch cleanup, while keeping default-branch pushes and Dolt synchronization separately authorized.
acceptance_criteria:
- The default bead-to-PR workflow explicitly triggers cleanup after the user reports review and merge.
- The agent verifies the PR merge before closing the bead.
- Cleanup covers fetching and pruning, safely reconciling local main, removing the clean bead worktree and feature branches, rerunning applicable merged-main checks, and reporting final status.
- Reachability and cleanliness checks precede forced worktree removal when a deleted upstream causes a false unpushed-commit warning.
- The instructions preserve local work and do not implicitly authorize pushing main or running bd dolt push.
notes: Implementation is review-ready. Summary: added a Post-Merge Cleanup trigger that verifies the hosted PR merge, closes the bead with the merge reference, preserves and reconciles local default-branch work, safely removes the merged worktree and branches, reruns applicable gates, and reports clean final state. The trigger authorizes scoped local closeout metadata commits and merged feature cleanup, but not pushing the default branch or running bd dolt push. Branch: bead/gpt-training-sandbox-dj8-post-merge-cleanup. Commit: 039cc3fccf61760c3af13001003844db882a77e5. Validation: red trigger search failed before the edit; required green trigger and safety text was found after; git diff --check passed; CLAUDE.md remains a valid symlink with identical content; 3 pytest smoke tests passed in an isolated environment; uv lock --check passed. Integration: no separate documentation integration suite or GitHub status checks exist. PR: https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/2. Risks/follow-up: none known; leave in_progress until the PR is confirmed merged.
close_reason: Merged via PR #2 (https://github.com/bjlkeng/gpt-training-sandbox/pull/2) as fb50e7134528e63da40a10441c808a4226ef38e7; implementation and validation evidence are preserved in the bead notes.

description:
Serve as the reviewable completion gate for this milestone. Delivery beads must be attached as blockers before any roadmap-decomposition placeholder is closed.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- ARC-Easy, ARC-Challenge, MMLU, GSM8K, and HumanEval-style evaluation run.
- ChatCORE and ChatCORE_cat are calculated and logged.
- Task and format results are persisted in chat_eval.json.

title: Phase 13: nanochat Architecture Parity
description:
Introduce RMSNorm, RoPE, ReLU-squared, QK norm, GQA, sliding windows, value embeddings, and experimental residual variants one at a time.

Plan ticket coverage: ARCH-001 through ARCH-012.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every feature has a config flag, unit/shape test, and tiny overfit test.
- Every feature records BPB and throughput comparisons.
- Unrelated architecture changes are not bundled into the same implementation bead.
- Ablation reports make the cause of metric changes attributable.
notes: Organizational roadmap container; executable work is tracked in child beads.

title: Milestone 9: Make It Fast Enough
description:
Serve as the reviewable completion gate for this milestone. Delivery beads must be attached as blockers before any roadmap-decomposition placeholder is closed.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- AMP, SDPA, activation checkpointing, and KV-cache inference are correct and benchmarked.
- Optional compile and FlashAttention paths fall back cleanly.
- Training and inference benchmark reports quantify the improvements.

title: Phase 12: Performance Optimizations
description:
Add AMP, SDPA, FlashAttention, compilation, activation checkpointing, and KV-cache inference only after the baseline is correct.

Plan ticket coverage: PERF-001 through PERF-014.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each optimized backend has a correctness comparison against the simple implementation.
- Fallback behavior is clean when optional acceleration is unavailable.
- Benchmarks show the speed or memory effect of each change.
- Cached greedy generation matches naive greedy generation.
notes: Organizational roadmap container; executable work is tracked in child beads.

title: Milestone 10: Architecture and Optimizer Experiments
description:
Serve as the reviewable completion gate for this milestone. Delivery beads must be attached as blockers before any roadmap-decomposition placeholder is closed.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- RMSNorm, RoPE, ReLU-squared, QK norm, GQA, and MuonAdamW experiments are represented by explicit beads.
- Each experiment has correctness, BPB, and throughput evidence.
- Ablation reports make comparisons reproducible.

title: Decide and decompose optional advanced chat work
description:
Progressively decompose the remaining ADV-001 through ADV-007 scope after Milestone 10 is complete; do not implement the whole range in this planning bead.

Plan ticket coverage: ADV-001 through ADV-007.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Review the current phase plan, completed code, cross-cutting tests, and metric contracts before defining work.
- Create coherent independently testable PR-sized child beads and preserve every listed source code exactly once.
- Give each child concrete acceptance criteria and only genuine technical dependencies.
- Record the decision and resulting dependencies on the phase epic before closing.
- Run bd dep cycles and confirm the newly exposed ready set remains small and actionable.
- Preference work must depend on the first chat model, tool parsing on the shared chat engine, and reward/eval work on ChatCORE.
- Do not create an executable tool-use bead without a separate approved sandboxing/security design.

title: Phase 15: Optional Advanced Chat Extensions
description:
Explore preference tuning, toy RL rewards, and tool-use parsing only after the base, SFT, and evaluation pipelines are stable.

Plan ticket coverage: ADV-001 through ADV-007.

Source of truth: plans/pre-training.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- Preference and reward data schemas are explicit and testable.
- Experimental trainers have bounded toy-domain evaluations.
- Tool execution is not enabled without a separate sandboxing and security design.
- Optional work can be deferred without blocking the core roadmap.
notes: Organizational roadmap container; executable work is tracked in child beads.
